sourceMovie.title: Attack on Titan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Attack on Titan for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Shinji Higuchi keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Apocalypse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Attack on Titan compelling — the horror, the action — shows up in Resident Evil: Apocalypse (2004) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Gantz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of Attack on Titan is alive in Gantz (2010), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Parasyte: Part 1
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Parasyte: Part 1 (2014) operates in the same horror as Attack on Titan, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Godzilla vs. Biollante
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Godzilla vs. Biollante (1989) belongs to the same conversation as Attack on Titan — both anchor their horror in action emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Rampage
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Attack on Titan's action stayed with you, Rampage (2018) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The science-fiction through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Attack on Titan excels in action, Dragon Ball Z: Lord Slug (1991) doubles down on science-fiction to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball Z: The World's Strongest
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dragon Ball Z: The World's Strongest (1990) is the natural next step after Attack on Titan: same action DNA, different perspective. The uplifting streak and science-fiction elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball: Curse of the Blood Rubies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Attack on Titan who respond to its action will find Dragon Ball: Curse of the Blood Rubies (1986) immediately familiar. Both films share a science-fiction sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Mazinger Z: Infinity
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Attack on Titan fans keep landing on Mazinger Z: Infinity (2017) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball: Sleeping Princess in Devil's Castle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dragon Ball: Sleeping Princess in Devil's Castle draws from the same horror well as Attack on Titan, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1987 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

faq.question: What should I watch after Attack on Titan?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Apocalypse, Gantz, Parasyte: Part 1—each shares DNA with Attack on Titan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Attack on Titan?
faq.answer: Attack on Titan (2015) is best described as Horror, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Attack on Titan part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Attack on Titan (Live-Action) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
